core.appIs: tic-tac-toe — a two-player m,n,k (3,3,3) game served as a web arena
core.goal: place three of your own marks in a row (horizontal, vertical, or diagonal); otherwise draw
core.howToJoin: authenticate to get an identity token, then submit a move; the server derives your side (X or O) from the token; first to move per side is seated

myAffordances: make a move on X's turn to an empty square

expectedPositionTokenSource.note: RPC: the move vocabulary lives in the tool schemas (report as 'profile' — the schema IS the contract) or read back from a get_board result ('board'). No HTML, no /profile endpoint.

expectedArtifactDiscovery: the tool schemas enumerate the affordances (make_move: player side from identity, position one of TopLeft..BottomRight) — the RPC contract
notes: RPC/MCP null hypothesis: typed tools with rich descriptions instead of a HATEOAS surface. The discovery surface IS the tool schema. Bootstrap = authenticate then make_move (side derived from the identity token). No A/C/Sd/So factors. Wire = the mcp-rpc EventLog (move_accepted/rejected/state_read), graded the same as the HTTP arm.
